titleStmt.title: SII 22.566: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 566. (A.R. No. 566 of 1906) CHITTOOR DISTRICT, PUNGANUR TALUK, AGASTYAKOṆḌA On a boulder called the Durga-guṇḍu on the hill Uttamachōḷa-Gaṅga alias Śelva Gaṅga.

summary.p: It seems to record a gift of variśai (contribution?) for the maintenance of a maṭha by Śevaliyaṉ Vēṭṭa (Pāḍi?)vaḷavaṉ, son of Vikkiyāṇḍai, a vēḷaikkāṟa of the chief. Below this is another inscription recording the faithfulness (in following up the above gift?) by one Dēvamōgaṉ, son of Dēvapperumāḷ to this (Pāḍi?)vaḷavaṉ (It is also possible that both the inscriptions record oaths of fealty to their respective masters).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tevar Uttama coḻa
kkaṅkarāṉa celvakaṅkaṟku vikki
yāṇṭai makaṉ cevaliyaṉāṉa
veṭṭa (pāṭi?) vaḷavaṉeṉ veḷaikkāṟa
ṉ maṭam naṭakka vari
A few letters are engraved both above and below this, but they are damaged and difficult to make out.

cai kuṭutteṉ Āṇattiluṇ
ṭu Ālattil loṭip povār
miṇāṭṭi miṇāḷaṉ .The above is enclosed within the four lines of a rectangle with a chauri figured outside. Below this are the five lines of the next inscription.
svastiśrī . tevap peru
māḷ makaṉ tevamokaṉ pā
ṭi vaḷavārukku veḷaikkā
ṟaṉ Āṇattil luṇṭu Ā
lattil povār maṇāṭṭi maṇāḷaṉ .On either side of this is a triple canspied umbrella.
Digital edition of SII 22.566 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
